recommendation_templates.department: Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence Laboratory
praise_phrases: one of the most intellectually curious students I have encountered in my 15 years of teaching
demonstrates exceptional analytical rigor and creativity in problem-solving
possesses a rare blend of technical mastery and collaborative spirit
work consistently exceeds expectations for graduate-level research
has the perseverance and insight of a seasoned researcher
academic_achievements: authored a conference paper on neural architecture search accepted at NeurIPS
developed a novel optimization algorithm that improved benchmark performance by 18%
led a team of four undergraduates to win the department's annual research showcase
maintained a perfect 4.0 GPA while conducting independent study in quantum machine learning
contributed significant code to an open-source deep learning framework with over 200 stars on GitHub

praise_phrases: a natural leader in the laboratory with an impeccable work ethic
ability to synthesize complex biological systems is truly outstanding
shows remarkable initiative and independence in experimental design
technical writing is as clear and compelling as that of a senior PhD candidate
intellectual maturity is well beyond their years
academic_achievements: first-authored a manuscript on CRISPR delivery systems currently under review at Nature Biotechnology
designed a microfluidic device for single-cell analysis that is now patented (USPTO #11,234,567)
awarded the prestigious Goldwater Scholarship for excellence in STEM research
presented original research at the annual meeting of the Biomedical Engineering Society
secured a competitive summer fellowship at the Broad Institute

praise_phrases: possesses an extraordinary talent for econometric modeling and causal inference
theoretical insights are both profound and pragmatically applicable
consistently produces work of publishable quality
has an exceptional ability to communicate complex economic concepts with clarity
research potential is, in my professional opinion, among the top 1% of undergraduates
academic_achievements: co-authored a working paper on labor market effects of automation disseminated by the NBER
won the department's Becker Prize for best undergraduate thesis on behavioral economics
developed a novel agent-based model to simulate policy impacts on wealth inequality
scored in the 99th percentile on the Economics GRE Subject Test
served as a teaching assistant for graduate-level econometrics, receiving perfect evaluations

recommendation_templates.department: Division of Physics, Mathematics and Astronomy
praise_phrases: mathematical intuition is nothing short of brilliant
approaches theoretical problems with a unique and powerful geometric perspective
tenacity in tackling open-ended research questions is inspiring
grasps advanced concepts in quantum field theory that typically challenge first-year graduate students
is destined to make significant contributions to fundamental physics
academic_achievements: derived an elegant solution to a long-standing problem in classical mechanics published in the American Journal of Physics
conducted summer research at CERN, contributing to data analysis for the ATLAS collaboration
awarded a national NSF REU grant for research on topological materials
achieved the highest score in the history of the department's graduate qualifying exam practice test
wrote a sophisticated simulation of gravitational wave signatures from black hole mergers

praise_phrases: a scholar of remarkable erudition and critical sophistication
prose is both analytically sharp and stylistically elegant
engages with literary theory in ways that are genuinely innovative
has the rare ability to make interdisciplinary connections that illuminate both fields
critical voice is already distinctive and authoritative
academic_achievements: senior thesis on postcolonial ecocriticism won the Hoopes Prize for outstanding scholarly work
presented a paper at the Modern Language Association annual convention as an undergraduate
published a review essay in a peer-reviewed journal, *Contemporary Literature*
designed and taught a voluntary seminar on speculative fiction for first-year students
fluent in four languages, enabling original research in French, German, and Spanish archives

praise_phrases: a phenomenal engineer with an intuitive grasp of complex fluid dynamics
practical problem-solving skills are matched by strong theoretical foundations
demonstrates exceptional leadership and project management in team settings
designs are not only functional but elegantly simple and robust
has the drive and vision to be a future pioneer in aerospace systems
academic_achievements: led a student team that placed 2nd nationally in the AIAA Design/Build/Fly competition
interned at SpaceX, contributing to CFD simulations for Starship re-entry heating
holds a provisional patent for a novel vortex lattice control surface
maintained a 3.98 GPA while serving as president of the student branch of AIAA
built and flight-tested a custom autonomous UAV for atmospheric data collection

recommendation_templates.department: School of Medicine, Department of Neuroscience
praise_phrases: scientific curiosity is boundless and deeply focused
shows exquisite care and precision in experimental methodology
ability to critically evaluate primary literature is at a doctoral level
thrives in the collaborative, fast-paced environment of a major research lab
has a genuine passion for translational research that bridges bench and bedside
academic_achievements: conducted independent research on synaptic plasticity, resulting in a co-authorship on a paper in *Neuron*
received the highest possible score on their senior honors thesis defense
mastered advanced techniques including in vivo two-photon calcium imaging and optogenetics
selected for the highly competitive Amgen Scholars Program
volunteered 200+ hours at a neurology clinic, demonstrating commitment to patient-oriented science

recommendation_templates.department: School of Computer Science, Human-Computer Interaction Institute
praise_phrases: a truly human-centered designer with exceptional empathy and technical skill
user studies are meticulously designed and yield profoundly insightful results
prototypes are both functionally sophisticated and aesthetically polished
understands the ethical dimensions of technology design with remarkable nuance
has the unique potential to shape the future of how humans interact with intelligent systems
academic_achievements: won a Best Paper award at the CHI Student Research Competition for work on accessible AR interfaces
designed an app for low-literacy populations that was piloted by a local nonprofit, serving 500+ users
led a semester-long project with a corporate partner (Google) that received a 'green light' for further development
built a fully functional multimodal (voice+gesture) interface for smart home control
published a critical analysis of algorithmic bias in a well-regarded HCI blog with significant academic readership
